question_text:
Find Leaves of Binary Tree

Given the root of a binary tree, collect a tree's nodes as if you were doing this:

  • Collect all the leaf nodes.
  • Remove all the leaf nodes.
  • Repeat until the tree is empty.

Return a 2D list of all removed leaf nodes in the order they were removed.
explanation: For each node, compute its "height" (distance to farthest leaf below). Nodes with height 0 are leaves, height 1 have only leaves as children, etc. Group nodes by their height. Use DFS returning height at each node; append node.val to result[height].

    pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
    if not data or data == ['']:
        sys.exit(0)
    args = [json.loads(line) for line in data]
    print(json.dumps(solve(*args)))
